title: Margin of Safety
subtitle: Risk-Averse Value Investing Strategies for the Thoughtful Investor
author: Seth A. Klarman
themes: Margin of safety as the central concept of sound investing
Risk is permanent loss of capital, not price volatility
Markets are often inefficient, especially in times of distress
Patience is the investor's most valuable asset
The auction process forces price discovery and discipline
Contrarianism earns its premium precisely because it is uncomfortable
Special situations and distressed securities offer structural mispricings
Forecasting is hubris; scenario analysis is humility
awards: Out of print for over two decades, with used copies commanding $1,000-$4,000
Frequently cited as one of the most important investing books ever written
Warren Buffett reportedly said he had read it twice
Reissued by HarperBusiness in 2013 with a new afterword by Klarman
excerpt: The three most important words in investing are 'margin of safety.' Originally a concept from Benjamin Graham, Seth Klarman elevates it into a complete investment philosophy: buy securities for no more than two-thirds of their intrinsic value, demand a buffer between price and value, and treat risk as the chance of permanent capital loss — not the daily up-and-down on a quote screen. After two decades out of print, the book has become a cult classic of value investing.
